questions.text: Who all lived in your household when you were growing up (parents, siblings, grandparents, aunts/uncles, others)?
What was the first name or nickname you used for each of your main caregivers?
What did a typical family dinner or evening meal look like — who sat where, what food, what conversations?
Did your family have any special sayings, nicknames, or inside jokes that were used all the time?
What was one chore or small responsibility you had around the house as a child?
Describe the feeling or sound of coming home after school or play — door opening, smells, voices.
Who in your family made you laugh the most — what kind of humour did they have?
What was your favourite family holiday, birthday or celebration memory from childhood?
Did anyone in your family have a special talent, hobby or skill that fascinated you?
How did people in your family show affection or care (words, hugs, gifts, doing things for each other…)?
Was there a small family tradition or ritual that meant a lot to you, even if it was very simple?
Was there ever a time you felt really protected, safe or deeply loved by your family?
Did you ever feel pressure to live up to a certain role or expectation in your family?
Was there a moment when you saw a parent, sibling or relative in a new light (more vulnerable, stronger, human)?
What was one time your family came together to support someone during a difficult moment?
Looking back, what is one thing about the way you were raised that you deeply appreciate today?
Was there a family conflict or difficult period that changed how you saw your family?
What is one thing you wish you had understood better about a family member when you were younger?
If you could go back and have one more ordinary day with your childhood family exactly as it was, what would that day be like?
What value or lesson did your family pass on to you — intentionally or just through how they lived?
Is there something you wish you had said to a family member while they were still here?
What part of your family upbringing do you most want to carry forward into your own relationships or family?
What part of your family upbringing do you consciously want to do differently if you have/had children?
Looking back, what one family member do you wish you could thank or apologise to today?
Is there a family story, secret or unspoken truth that shaped how your family functioned?
What do you hope your own children or future generations understand about where you came from?
If your family could hear one honest message from your adult heart, what would you say?
